component_identification.content: Agricola includes player farmyards, action boards, major improvement board, fences, stables, rooms, family members, resource tokens, grain and vegetable markers, animal tokens, and large occupation and minor improvement card sets. The game runs over fourteen rounds and six harvest stages using a fixed round card schedule. Players begin with small farms and family workers and expand through actions and card effects.

core_game_loop.content: Each round reveals a new action space and refills accumulation spaces. Players place one family member at a time on open actions and resolve immediately. After all placements, workers return home. At harvest rounds, resolve fields, feed family members, then breeding. The game ends after round fourteen and final harvest.

detailed_rules.content: Worker placement spaces are generally exclusive. Actions provide resources, plow and sow, build rooms and fences, take animals, play cards, and grow family. Family growth gives more actions later but increases feeding burden. Fields must be plowed before sowing and then yield over future harvests. Animals require legal housing in pastures, stables, or allowed home spaces; excess animals are lost. Fences must form complete enclosures and require wood at build time. Improvements and occupations create food conversion engines and action discounts. Feeding phase penalties are severe through begging markers when food is short. Breeding gives at most one offspring per species when at least two are present and legal room exists. Common mistakes are illegal animal storage, delayed food planning, and overcommitting to family growth before a stable feeding engine exists.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Final scoring is category based and rewards balanced development across fields, pastures, crops, animals, rooms, family, and improvements while penalizing neglected categories and begging markers. There is no early trigger; score only after round fourteen final harvest. Highest points wins using rulebook tie breaks.

beginner_strategy.content: Build a food engine early. Grow family only when feeding is secure. Use wood efficiently by fencing in planned chunks. Avoid negative categories before pushing big combos. Read your starting cards for synergy and commit to a coherent path.

metadata.notes: Rewrite sprint with rulebook-text as primary source.
